title: Common wire types, proto<->core conversions, and the WireError taxonomy
purpose: Define the shared primitives every other message family uses: the proto representations of aion-core's identifiers, payload, and status; the lossless conversions between proto and aion-core types; and the WireError taxonomy that turns the engine's and store's typed errors into stable, language-neutral codes.
task: Add common.proto declaring proto messages for WorkflowId, RunId, ActivityId, TimerId, Payload, WorkflowStatus, and a thin envelope that wraps a serialised aion-core value with a namespace and request id. Write convert.rs giving infallible-where-possible, lossless conversions between these proto messages and aion-core types. Define WireError as a closed taxonomy with stable codes and write the mapping from aion's EngineError, aion-store's StoreError, and query/signal/namespace failures onto it. Reuse aion-core's Event/WorkflowFilter/WorkflowSummary directly via the envelope — do not clone them as wire structs.

requirements.title: Common proto messages and core conversions
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define proto messages for WorkflowId, RunId, ActivityId, TimerId, Payload, and WorkflowStatus, and SHALL provide lossless conversion between each and its aion-core counterpart.
acceptance: common.proto defines messages mirroring aion-core WorkflowId, RunId, ActivityId, TimerId, Payload, and WorkflowStatus.
convert.rs implements From/TryFrom both directions for each type; a unit test round-trips every type proto->core->proto and core->proto->core and asserts equality.
Payload conversion preserves the content-type tag and bytes exactly (lossless for all JSON value kinds, mirroring aion-core's Payload guarantee).
Conversions that cannot fail use From; conversions that can (malformed proto) use TryFrom returning a WireError.

requirements.title: Envelope reuses aion-core types; no wire clones
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL carry aion-core Event, WorkflowFilter, and WorkflowSummary across the wire via a thin envelope (namespace, request id, serialised aion-core value), NOT via re-declared wire-clone structs.
acceptance: A WireEnvelope (or equivalent) carries a namespace, an optional request id, and a serialised aion-core value (e.g. a Payload-encoded or bytes-encoded core type).
Event, WorkflowFilter, and WorkflowSummary are serialised from aion-core's own serde derives, not from any wire-specific duplicate type.
A grep/inspection confirms aion-proto declares no struct that re-fields Event, WorkflowFilter, WorkflowSummary, or WorkflowStatus (per CO12).
A unit test round-trips an aion-core Event of at least one variant through the envelope and asserts equality.

requirements.title: WireError closed taxonomy
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define WireError as a closed taxonomy carrying a stable code and a human message, covering not-found, namespace-denied, sequence-conflict, unknown-query, query-timeout, not-running, lagged, and backend categories.
acceptance: WireError has variants (with stable integer or string codes) for NotFound, NamespaceDenied, SequenceConflict, UnknownQuery, QueryTimeout, NotRunning, Lagged, and Backend.
WireError derives Serialize + Deserialize, implements std::error::Error and Display, and is Send + Sync.
Each code is documented as stable so client SDKs may branch on it; the human message is informational only.
WireError is representable as a proto message in common.proto and round-trips losslessly.

requirements.title: Mapping engine and store errors onto WireError
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL map aion's EngineError, aion-store's StoreError, and query/signal/namespace failures onto WireError, never exposing Debug-formatted internals on the wire.
acceptance: A documented mapping turns each EngineError and StoreError variant into a specific WireError code (e.g. StoreError::SequenceConflict -> WireError::SequenceConflict, EngineError unknown-workflow -> NotFound).
Namespace authorisation failures map to NamespaceDenied; query timeout and unknown-query map to QueryTimeout and UnknownQuery; a terminated/unknown workflow maps to NotRunning.
No mapping path forwards a Debug or Display of the underlying error verbatim as the code; the human message is the only free-text field (per D3).
A unit test asserts representative mappings for an EngineError and a StoreError variant.

The mapping lives in aion-proto and references aion-store's StoreError; it references aion's EngineError type only structurally — note that if EngineError is not reachable from a leaf crate, the mapping for engine errors is provided as a documented conversion the server (AW-008/AW-009) applies, and aion-proto owns only the StoreError + namespace + query/signal mappings. State which split is used.

boundaries: SHALL NOT define the workflow, events, or worker service messages — AW-003, AW-004, AW-005.
SHALL NOT depend on aion (the engine crate); if EngineError mapping cannot live in a leaf crate, the engine-error mapping is applied server-side and documented as such (R4 records the split).
SHALL NOT re-declare Event/WorkflowFilter/WorkflowSummary/WorkflowStatus as wire-clone structs (CO12).
SHALL NOT implement any server handler or transport — those are AW-008 onward.
verification: No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
Inspection confirms no wire-clone of aion-core's Event/Filter/Summary/Status types exists.
